_comment: Tests listed here are known to diverge from Linux. They still run and are reported as XFAIL, but do not fail the suite. Remove entries as implementations are completed.
subsystems.ns_uts: unshare(CLONE_NEWUTS) requires CAP_SYS_ADMIN on Linux but Kevlar does not enforce capability checks yet. The contract runner is unprivileged.
vm.mremap_xfail: Old test prints address which differs due to ASLR; replaced by vm.mremap_grow.
events.epoll_oneshot_xfail: EPOLLONESHOT implemented but test times out in QEMU (pre-existing epoll_wait blocking issue with pipes). Investigate POLL_WAIT_QUEUE wakeup path.
signals.sigaltstack_xfail: sigaltstack is a stub returning Ok(0); SA_ONSTACK has no effect. Tracked for M9.
process.setuid_roundtrip: Kevlar runs as root (uid=0/gid=0) vs Linux contract runner as user (uid=1000/gid=1001); test passes on both (test artifact).
process.execve_argv_envp: Serial captures parent printf before execve; not present in Linux captured output (test artifact).
process.setsid_session: Child _exit() skips stdio flush on Linux; Kevlar serial captures all writes (test artifact).
process.wait4_wnohang: Child PID=2 on Kevlar vs high PID on Linux; PID values are non-deterministic (test artifact).
threading.tgkill_self: TID=1 on Kevlar vs high TID on Linux; TID values are non-deterministic (test artifact).
time.clock_realtime: Wall-clock tv_sec differs by 1s due to sequential test execution timing (test artifact).
